commit.message:
fix: suppress banner for Cobra internal __complete commands

PersistentPreRun previously only skipped ui.Header() for the user-visible
'completion' subcommand. Cobra also dispatches internal '__complete' and
'__completeNoDesc' commands when shell scripts request live completions
(e.g. pressing TAB in fish). Those calls also write to stdout, so the banner
would appear inside the completion response, corrupting fish's display.

Extend the guard to also skip PersistentPreRun when cmd.Name() is
'__complete' or '__completeNoDesc'.

fix: extract JSON object from model response to handle trailing prose

When tool-calling mode is active, ResponseMIMEType: "application/json"
cannot be set (Gemini SDK incompatibility). Without that constraint,
Gemini may append preamble or postamble text around the JSON object,
e.g. trailing version notes or a 'Note:' sentence starting with 'v'.

Add extractJSONObject() helper that slices from the first '{' to the
last '}', stripping any surrounding non-JSON content before unmarshal.

Fixes: unmarshal error 'invalid character v after object key:value pair'
seen during Credit Karma Istio migration video analysis.

Phase 1: compiler-rt + newlib migration (submodule updates and cxxnew fixup)

Bump submodule pointers and fix operator new/delete mangled names to match
the corrected SizeType = UnsignedLong in Clang's S1C33 TargetInfo.

tools/crt/cxxnew.c:
  SizeType was changed from UnsignedInt ('j') to UnsignedLong ('m') in
  S1C33TargetInfo to match gcc33's ILP32 ABI and newlib's size_t.  The
  C++ Itanium ABI mangles size_t-sized parameters with the type's mangled
  letter, so all operator new/delete entry points must be renamed:
    _Znwj  → _Znwm   (operator new(size_t))
    _Znaj  → _Znam   (operator new[](size_t))
    _ZdlPvj → _ZdlPvm (operator delete(void*, size_t))
    _ZdaPvj → _ZdaPvm (operator delete[](void*, size_t))
    _ZnwjRKSt9nothrow_t → _ZnwmRKSt9nothrow_t
    _ZnajRKSt9nothrow_t → _ZnamRKSt9nothrow_t
  The local typedef is also corrected to 'unsigned long' for consistency.

.gitmodules:
  Switch newlib remote from the upstream sourceware.org read-only mirror
  to git@github.com:autch/newlib-s1c33.git so the S1C33 machine support
  patches (ieeefp.h, setjmp.h, machine/s1c33/) can be pushed and tracked.

llvm  → c448351627e8 (S1C33: add compiler-rt builtins and i64/ConstantPool lowering)
newlib → 824c1eb0d688 (newlib: add S1C33 machine support)

crt: remove -flto=full from cxxrt.c and cxxnew.c

Runtime stubs must be native ELF objects, not LTO bitcode.  LLVM LTO
treats certain well-known functions (abort, memcpy, etc.) as external
references even when they are defined in LTO bitcode, leaving symbols
unresolved in the LTO output.  Compiling all of libcxxrt.a without LTO
avoids this class of problem entirely.

halt.c was already native; now cxxrt.c and cxxnew.c are too.

commit.message:
crt: add halt.c with native abort()/_exit() to fix LTO linker error

LLVM LTO treats abort() as a well-known external library function and
emits an external call in the LTO output even when abort() is defined
in LTO bitcode (e.g. in cxxrt.c compiled with -flto=full).  This caused
a linker error when cpptest was built with -flto=full:

  newlib stdlib.h declares abort() as __attribute__((noreturn))
  → LTO optimizer transforms AppBase::~AppBase() [D0Ev, mustBeUnreachable]
    to call abort()
  → abort.o from liblib.a is extracted to satisfy abort reference
  → abort.o calls _exit, which is not in the sysroot

Fix: introduce tools/crt/halt.c compiled WITHOUT -flto=full.  The
resulting native ELF abort() in halt.o satisfies the linker without
requiring liblib.a's abort.o, severing the abort→_exit dependency chain.
_exit() is provided as a weak symbol so application code can override it.

Also fix cpptest.cpp's _exit signature: void _exit(void) → void _exit(int)
to match the POSIX / newlib declaration.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add vger track digest with Google Genkit Go (Phase 3)

Introduce Google Genkit Go for the multi-step track digest pipeline.
This is the first use of Genkit in vger — existing commands are unchanged.

New command:
  vger track digest [--status] [--enrich] [--output <file>]

Pipeline:
  1. Load signals (filtered by --status / --category)
  2. Optional batch enrich via direct Gemini SDK (--enrich flag)
  3. Synthesise via Genkit DigestSignalsFlow (1 Gemini call, typed output)
  4. Render to terminal + optional Markdown file (--output)

New files:
  internal/adapters/genkit/digest.go  — DigestSignals() wraps a DefineFlow
    that calls genkit.GenerateData[SignalDigestReport] for type-safe output
  internal/cli/track_digest.go        — cobra command with full rendering

Domain additions (model.go):
  SignalDigestReport, FocusItem, TechCluster, SignalPulse — with jsonschema tags
  for Genkit's typed output schema generation

New dependencies (go.mod):
  github.com/firebase/genkit/go v1.6.0
  github.com/firebase/genkit/go/plugins/googlegenai (via mod tidy)
  github.com/google/dotprompt/go v0.0.0-20251014011017-8d056e027254
  github.com/invopop/jsonschema v0.13.0

Why Genkit here (not earlier):
  - track digest is the first multi-step pipeline with typed structured output
  - genkit.GenerateData[T] eliminates manual JSON parsing + fence trimming
  - The dev UI (genkit start) enables prompt iteration without rebuilding

docs: update dataflow.md to reflect hybrid ReAct architecture

Document the implemented hybrid approach: Go-controlled outer pipeline
with Gemini native function-calling inner loop inside AnalyseVideo.

Key clarifications:
- Outer pipeline (FetchMetadata → AnalyseVideo) stays in Go because
  these steps are always required in the same order, and Go can check
  the cache before any LLM cost is incurred
- AnalyseVideo IS the primary Gemini call — it cannot be wrapped as a
  tool inside another Gemini session (no nesting possible)
- The function-calling loop (lookup_cncf_project, validate_url) covers
  exactly the non-deterministic enrichment where LLM autonomy adds value

fix: switch API tsconfig to ESNext+bundler for shared extensionless imports

The API tsconfig used NodeNext moduleResolution which requires .js
extensions in all relative imports. Since packages/shared was switched to
bundler resolution (extensionless imports for Turbopack compat), the API
typecheck failed when processing shared source files via the path alias.

Changing the API to module:ESNext+moduleResolution:bundler resolves this:
- Bundler resolution accepts both extensionless and .js imports
- Existing API source .js extensions are still valid
- Shared source extensionless imports now compile without TS2835 errors
- Next.js Turbopack remains happy with extensionless shared imports

commit.message:
fix(ci): gitleaks with fallback to full scan when base SHA is unreachable (closes #3)

* fix(ci): replace gitleaks-action with direct install to handle missing base SHA (closes #3)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ci): correct tar stdin flag for gitleaks binary extraction

tar xz gitleaks -C /tmp reads from a file named 'gitleaks', not stdin.
Use tar -xzf - -C /tmp gitleaks to explicitly read from stdin (-f -)
and extract only the gitleaks member to /tmp.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ci): use bash array for gitleaks SCAN_ARGS to prevent word-splitting

Using a plain string SCAN_ARGS and unquoted $SCAN_ARGS expansion causes
shell word-splitting: '--log-opts=--no-merges --first-parent BASE..HEAD'
splits into three tokens, making '--first-parent' an unknown gitleaks flag.

Switch to a bash array so gitleaks receives '--log-opts' and the full
'--no-merges --first-parent BASE..HEAD' string as two properly-quoted args.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
